title: Fujifilm Joins "FRY to FLY" Project to Supply UCO for SAF Production
content: FUJIFILM Holdings has announced its participation in the "FRY to FLY Project," a collaborative initiative in Japan aiming to produce Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) from domestic bio-resources. As part of this commitment, Fujifilm will collect used cooking oil (UCO) from all its employee cafeterias across the group in Japan and supply it as feedstock for SAF production. This group-wide rollout follows a successful pilot started at the Yokohama Minato Center in June 2024. By recycling waste oil that would otherwise be discarded, the company contributes to a circular economy and the decarbonization of the aviation sector. This initiative aligns with Fujifilm's "Sustainable Value Plan 2030," which prioritizes climate action and resource circulation. The project helps establish a stable domestic supply chain for cleaner fuel, reducing lifecycle CO2 emissions.
author: Analysts Account
